In summary, we describe here a protocol of modified PCR-based site-directed mutagenesis used to splice to- gether different regions of a gene in a one-tube PCR. In comparison with methods now available for assembling DNA segments of a gene in tandem, it is simple, conve- nient, and time-saving. As no nearby restriction sites are re- quired or engineered into the mutagenic primer, a deletion can be introduced anywhere within the sequence of inter- est. If more than three DNA segments of a gene are to be assembled in tandem, one more round of PCR mutagen- esis with the desired mutagenic primer should be performed using the mutant plasmid DNA obtained from the last round of PCR mutagenesis as template. We routinely use this procedure in our laboratory with great success.
